---
title: "credits.aleo"
subtitle: "The native ALEO token, walked through line by line."
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status:** Stub. Full chapter coming soon.
:::

## The question

Every Aleo dApp imports `credits.aleo` for fee payment, balance transfers,
and as the canonical example of "how Aleo programs are written." But few
developers have actually read its source. What does the deployed contract
actually contain?

## What this chapter will cover

- All 7 transfer functions (`transfer_public`, `transfer_private`, the 4
  bridges, etc.) walked through with diagrams
- The `credits` record: what fields it has, why
- The `account` mapping: what's public, what isn't
- The `bond_public` / `unbond_public` / `claim_unbond_public` staking flow
- Annotated bytecode: what each AVM instruction does in the compiled output
- Side-by-side: source Leo on the left, AVM bytecode on the right, hover any
  line to highlight the correspondence

## Interactive viz planned

The bytecode walker: pick a function, see the AVM instructions execute
step-by-step against a sample input. Show the records consumed and produced
at each step.

## Why this matters

`credits.aleo` is the **reference implementation** for every other Aleo token.
Once you understand its 7 transfer variants, you understand:

- ARC-20 token contracts via `token_registry.aleo`
- Stablecoins (USDCx, USAD) — same shape, plus compliance hooks
- Pondo's pALEO — built on top of credits

This is the "read the standard library before writing your own" chapter.
